name: Mara Ellison
role: Archivist (between jobs), returning to Greyhaven to settle her grandmother's estate
backstory: Grew up visiting Greyhaven in summers. Left for university and work cataloging collections. Lost her job after the archives closed. Grandmother recently passed, leaving her keys, boxes, and a quiet injunction to 'remember rightly.'
desires: to do right by her grandmother
to keep a tidy boundary around grief
fears: belonging to a place that can also lose her

relationships.Eloise Hart: Respected town voice; Mara grew up listening to Eloise's show. Mara brings Eloise a letter addressed to her.
relationships.Jonah Pike: Quiet lighthouse caretaker; initial friction softens into trust as they investigate together.

secrets: She almost deleted a digital collection during a server migration and never confessed; the guilt makes her cautious with memory.
She once mailed herself a letter to test a dead-letter office theory she found in a book; it never arrived.
arc_beats: Begins in withdrawal, treating the town as an archive to be cataloged.
Allows herself to participate, not just record—first at the radio studio, later at the lighthouse.
Becomes a witness for truth rather than its judge, recognizing repair as work done in the open.

name: Jonah Pike
role: Fisherman and volunteer caretaker of Bellwether Light
backstory: Arrived in Greyhaven in his late teens, took odd jobs, learned the coastline. Lives above a boat shed across from the decommissioned lighthouse. Keeps tools in order, repairs what others overlook.
desires: to keep the town safe in quiet ways
to avoid dredging up old tragedies
to stay near the sea without naming why
fears: being recognized for what he was, not who he is
hurting the town again by telling the full story

relationships.Mara Ellison: Sees her listening more than speaking; slowly trusts her with practical history.
relationships.Eloise Hart: Respects her discipline; they share certain late-night silences.

secrets: He is living under a borrowed name; his real name is Rowan Hart.
On the night the light went dark, he made a choice that prevented a larger catastrophe but left him unable to keep his old life.
arc_beats: Holds his name like a sealed letter.
Reveals himself not under pressure, but because someone deserves the truth.
Remains in Greyhaven under his chosen name, seen and accepted.

name: Eloise Hart
role: Late-night radio host of 'Night Tides' on WGHN; Rowan Hart's older sister
backstory: Born and raised in Greyhaven. On air since her twenties. The night the light went dark, she took a call she later cut from the broadcast log. She has kept a key to the lighthouse lens room for years.
desires: to hold the town's story without breaking it
to know when silence serves and when it destroys
fears: that her discretion harmed people she meant to help
that the town will turn story into cudgel

relationships.Mara Ellison: Sees her as a careful listener who might carry a truth without sensationalizing.
relationships.Jonah Pike: Knows his cadence too well; she suspected long ago who he was.

secrets: She recognized Jonah as Rowan but chose not to expose him, believing repair would take time and quiet work.
She keeps the off-air tape from September 17, 1994.
arc_beats: Guards the past behind a practiced voice.
Chooses disclosure at the right moment, with the right witness present.
Returns to air with humility about the limits of keeping and telling.
